This is especially useful in writing, naming, interface copy, visual directions, and early product thinking. The model should produce diversity, not just paraphrases. Different tones, structures, levels of ambition, and risk profiles. Ideally each option makes a distinct bet.

A good system, then, does three things:
- generates multiple candidates by default
- makes the differences legible
- lets me merge, mutate, and request another round

I trust AI most when it helps me explore. I trust humans most when a choice needs taste, context, or responsibility. So the split of labor feels obvious: let intelligence do the branching; let the person do the choosing.

title.zh-CN: 使用 Playwright 和 BeautifulSoup 提取渲染后的文本
title.en: Rendered text extraction with Playwright and BeautifulSoup

summary.zh-CN: 对于高度依赖 JavaScript 的页面，最简单且可靠的做法通常是：先让浏览器完成渲染，再解析最终得到的 HTML。它并不是完美的正文提取器，但作为第一步非常实用。
summary.en: For JavaScript-heavy pages, the simplest reliable move is often: let a browser render first, then parse the resulting HTML. It is not a perfect main-content extractor, but it is a very practical first pass.

body.zh-CN:
有些页面在 JavaScript 执行完成之前，实际上并不存在。对于这类页面，`requests` + BeautifulSoup 很优雅，但往往并不完整：页面外壳到了，正文没到。我目前低摩擦的做法是先让 Playwright 把页面渲染出来，拿到最终 HTML，再用 BeautifulSoup 把 `<body>` 展平成文本。

```python
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def fetch_text(url: str) -> str:
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch()
        page = browser.new_page()
        page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle")
        html = page.content()
        browser.close()
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.body or soup
    return "\n".join(body.stripped_strings)
```

我喜欢这种做法的原因：
- **它适用于客户端渲染页面。**
- **它容易检查和调试。**
- **它能快速给我一份可用的文本输出**，通常已经足够用于索引、摘要生成或笔记采集。

但这是一把钝器。它提取的是 **全部** body 文本，而不是任何语义意义上的“正文”。导航、页脚文本、推荐内容，以及重复的 UI 标签都会被一并带上。对于一次性的抓取，这种权衡完全可以接受。若要更高质量的提取，我会加上定向选择器、样板内容剔除，或者做一遍 Readability 风格的处理。

对我最初草稿做一个小修正：在 Playwright Python 里，`page.text()` 不是我这里想要调用的方法。真正有用的是 `page.content()`，因为页面一旦渲染完成，HTML 才是更稳定的解析对象。

**参考**
- 示例页面: https://36kr.com/p/2689307678240384
body.en:
Some pages do not really exist until JavaScript finishes. For those, `requests` + BeautifulSoup is elegant but often incomplete: the shell arrives, the article does not. My current low-friction pattern is to let Playwright render the page, grab the final HTML, and then flatten the `<body>` into text with BeautifulSoup.

```python
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def fetch_text(url: str) -> str:
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch()
        page = browser.new_page()
        page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle")
        html = page.content()
        browser.close()
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.body or soup
    return "\n".join(body.stripped_strings)
```

Why I like this approach:
- **It works on client-rendered pages.**
- **It is easy to inspect and debug.**
- **It gives me a usable text dump quickly**, which is often enough for indexing, summarization, or note capture.

But this is a blunt tool. It extracts **all** body text, not the “main article” in any semantic sense. Navigation, footer text, recommendations, and repeated UI labels will come along for the ride. For a one-off scrape, that tradeoff is fine. For higher-quality extraction, I would add targeted selectors, boilerplate removal, or a Readability-style pass.

One small correction to my original sketch: in Playwright Python, `page.text()` is not the call I want here. The useful step is `page.content()`, because once the page is rendered, HTML is the stable thing to parse.

**Reference**
- Example page: https://36kr.com/p/2689307678240384

title.zh-CN: 我真正想从聊天助手那里得到什么
title.en: What I Actually Want from a Chat Assistant

summary.zh-CN: 一份简短的产品备忘录：我想要的助手能做对话分析、回复起草、自定义记忆，以及合理的异步处理，而不会抹掉人与人交流的质感。
summary.en: A small product memo: the assistant I want does conversation analysis, reply drafting, custom memory, and sane async handling without stripping away the human texture.

body.zh-CN:
我总会回到同一个产品直觉上：聊天助手不该只会回应提示。它应该帮我消化一段对话，提炼真正重要的内容，并把杂乱的交流整理成可用的结构，同时又不把其中的人味抹平。

**我希望它做到的事**
- **对话快照**：话题概览、参与者，以及每个人的核心观点。
- **分歧地图**：分歧在哪里，头脑风暴从哪里分叉，以及哪些问题仍然悬而未决。
- **行动提取**：我需要跟进的事项、待解答的问题，以及具体的待办事项。
- **上下文层**：对生僻词、领域术语和命名实体的快速解释。
- **联网模式**：当本地上下文不够时，提供更深入、可联网的自由对话。
- **写作支持**：建议回复、调整语气、打磨措辞，同时又不让所有内容都带上机器生成的腔调。
- **个性化关注**：让我定义自己在意的点，然后在跨对话场景中可靠地提取并追踪这些自定义关注项。

这里还有一个虽小但重要的系统层备注：长时间运行的请求应该以异步方式提交，并通过轮询获取结果，而不是假装单个 URL 请求能扛过一分钟超时。产品质量往往就藏在这些看似无聊的边缘细节里。

所以，这不只是一份功能清单，而是一种设计立场：把对话视为可用于摘要、观点提炼、分歧分析、记忆与行动的原材料。理想的助手应该一部分像分析师，一部分像编辑，一部分像协作者——知道何时该结构化，何时该解释，又何时只是单纯地帮我把回复写得更好。
body.en:
I keep returning to the same product instinct: a chat assistant should do more than answer prompts. It should help me digest a conversation, surface what matters, and turn messy dialogue into usable structure without flattening the human texture.

**What I want it to do**
- **Conversation snapshot**: topic overview, who participated, and each person’s core view.
- **Tension map**: where the disagreements are, where the brainstorm branched, and what still feels unresolved.
- **Action extraction**: my follow-ups, open questions, and concrete TODOs.
- **Context layer**: quick explanations for rare words, domain terms, and named entities.
- **Networked mode**: a deeper, web-enabled freeform conversation when local context is not enough.
- **Writing support**: suggest replies, adjust tone, and tighten wording without making everything sound like AI.
- **Personal attention**: let me define what I care about, then reliably extract and track those custom concerns across chats.

There is also a small but important systems note here: long-running requests should be submitted asynchronously and retrieved by polling, rather than pretending a single URL request can survive a one-minute timeout. Product quality is often hidden in these boring edges.

So this is not just a feature list. It is a design stance: treat conversation as raw material for summaries, viewpoints, disagreement analysis, memory, and action. The ideal assistant feels part analyst, part editor, part collaborator — and knows when to structure, when to explain, and when to simply help me reply better.

title.zh-CN: 一条流水线统一处理聊天截图、历史导出和实时微信消息
title.en: One Pipeline for Chat Screenshots, History Dumps, and Live WeChat Messages

summary.zh-CN: 用 Moonshot 将聊天截图 OCR 转成结构化会话记录，再结合 Wechaty 的实时摄取与 WeChat dump 的历史回填，汇入一个可查询的记忆层。
summary.en: Use Moonshot to turn chat screenshot OCR into structured conversation records, then combine that with Wechaty live ingestion and WeChat dump backfill into a single queryable memory layer.

body.zh-CN:
**想法**

我总会回到同一个模式：聊天历史通常以两种都不太方便的形态存在——截图 OCR 对应混乱的过去，消息流对应干净的未来。真正有用的系统，大概是一条同时处理这两者的流水线。

Moonshot 很适合处理截图这一侧。不只是做简单的 OCR 清洗，而是把 OCR 输出转成结构化的会话对象：说话人、时间戳、引用回复、话题切换、行动项，以及不确定性标记。一旦结构存在，更有意思的一步就是 **深入解释**：这里到底发生了什么，做出了什么决定，缺了什么，隐含了哪些后续跟进。

**采集策略**

- **历史回填：** 尽可能使用 WeChat dump 同步历史聊天数据。
- **增量摄取：** 使用 Wechaty 作为云端消息消费者接收新消息。
- **兜底方案：** 当唯一留下的载体是截图时，先跑 OCR，再让 Moonshot 做规范化和解释。

关键在于把这三种输入收敛到同一个 schema。由截图提取出的消息，应该在形态上与原生消息一致，只是置信度更低，并且带有更强的溯源链接，指回对应的图像裁剪片段。

**关键点**

我不会为“完美还原”做优化。我会为 **可搜索的会话记忆** 做优化：结构足够用于索引，证据足够用于核验，模型推理能力足够用于总结或解释。

难点其实很明确：OCR 噪声、元数据缺失、图片顺序、内容重复，以及隐私边界。因此模型应该标注不确定性，绝不能默默虚构缺失字段，并始终将原始证据保留在结果旁边。

这与其说是在做聊天归档，不如说是在为碎片化的会话历史构建一个真正可用的接口。
body.en:
**Idea**

I keep coming back to the same pattern: chat history exists in two inconvenient forms — screenshot OCR for the messy past, and message streams for the clean future. The useful system is probably one pipeline that handles both.

Moonshot feels like a good fit for the screenshot side. Not just plain OCR cleanup, but turning OCR output into a structured conversation object: speakers, timestamps, quoted replies, topic shifts, action items, and uncertainty markers. Once the structure exists, the more interesting step is **drill-down interpretation**: ask what happened here, what decision was made, what is missing, what follow-up is implied.

**Capture strategy**

- **Backfill:** use a WeChat dump to sync historical chat data where possible.
- **Incremental ingestion:** use Wechaty as a cloud message consumer for new messages.
- **Fallback:** when the only artifact is a screenshot, run OCR, then let Moonshot normalize and interpret it.

The key is to converge all three inputs into the same schema. A screenshot-derived message should look like a native message, just with lower confidence and stronger provenance links back to the image crops.

**What matters**

I would not optimize for perfect reconstruction. I would optimize for **searchable conversational memory**: enough structure to index, enough evidence to verify, enough model reasoning to summarize or explain.

The hard parts are predictable: OCR noise, missing metadata, image order, duplicated content, and privacy boundaries. So the model should annotate uncertainty, never silently invent missing fields, and always keep the raw evidence nearby.

This feels less like chat archiving and more like building a usable interface over fragmented conversation history.

title.zh-CN: 聊天作为 AI 的默认界面，其实很弱
title.en: Chat Is a Weak Default Interface for AI

summary.zh-CN: 聊天作为通用兜底方式很好用，但对于可重复的 AI 任务，它并不适合作为主要交互面。具备上下文感知、能主动呈现的交互入口，可以缩短触发路径、降低使用成本，并帮助用户形成习惯。
summary.en: Chat works well as a universal fallback, but it is a poor primary surface for repeatable AI tasks. Context-aware, proactive affordances shorten the trigger path, reduce effort, and help usage become habit.

body.zh-CN:
聊天是一个很好的兜底入口，但我反复回到同一个产品直觉：它作为 AI 的默认界面其实很弱。

在 IM 风格的 UI 中，每项能力一开始都是隐藏的。用户必须先记得这个功能存在，意识到当下正适合使用它，然后再组织出能把它激活的提示词。于是，记忆、时机和措辞，都成了使用产品成本的一部分。对日常性任务来说，这个成本太高了。

这就是为什么很多 AI 功能在演示里看起来惊艳，却在日常使用中诡异地缺席。模型也许很强，但触发路径太长。

**我会优先优化什么**
- 在用户开口前先展示可能需要的操作
- 从上下文推断意图，而不只是依赖输入的命令
- 让第一个有用步骤只需一次点击，而不是写上一整段话
- 把聊天保留为兜底方式，而不是唯一入口

例子都很简单：总结这个线程，起草一条回复，提取行动项，把这个文件和上一版做对比，把这条笔记转成 ticket。如果系统已经能足够可靠地推断出这些时刻，就应该主动把它们呈现出来。

产品目标不只是准确率，而是 **启动能量**。更短的触发路径会降低执行成本；更低的成本会增加重复使用；重复会变成习惯。一旦某种行为变成习惯，AI 就不再像一个“特殊工具”，而更像环境的一部分。

这种转变——从等待被调用，到主动给出下一个有用动作——看起来比单靠聊天更像一种更持久的交互模型。
body.en:
Chat is a great escape hatch, but I keep coming back to the same product intuition: it is a weak default interface for AI.

In an IM-style UI, every capability starts hidden. The user has to remember that a function exists, notice that the current moment is a good fit, and then compose a prompt that activates it. That means recall, timing, and wording all become part of the cost of using the product. For routine tasks, that cost is too high.

This is why many AI features feel impressive in demos and strangely absent in daily use. The model may be powerful, but the trigger path is long.

**What I’d optimize for**
- show likely actions before the user asks
- derive intent from context, not just from typed commands
- make the first useful step one tap, not one paragraph
- keep chat as a fallback, not the only doorway

Examples are simple: summarize this thread, draft a reply, extract action items, compare this file with the last version, turn this note into a ticket. If the system can infer these moments reliably enough, it should present them proactively.

The product goal is not just accuracy. It is **activation energy**. Shorter trigger paths lower execution cost; lower cost increases repetitions; repetitions become habit. Once a behavior becomes habitual, the AI stops feeling like a special tool and starts feeling like part of the environment.

That shift—from waiting to be invoked to offering the next useful move—feels like a more durable interaction model than chat alone.

title.zh-CN: 中国手机 AI：苹果的合规缺口可能正是机会窗口
title.en: China Mobile AI: Apple’s Compliance Gap Might Be the Opening

summary.zh-CN: 一个用于更深入审视手机 AI 的工作假设：在中国，赢家的决定因素可能与其说是模型质量，不如说是合规、本地整合和默认分发能力。如果苹果的 AI 技术栈遭遇类似 OpenAI 的监管摩擦，国内 OEM 可能会获得一个真正的窗口期。
summary.en: A working hypothesis for a deeper scan of handset AI: in China, the winner may be defined less by model quality than by compliance, local integration, and default distribution. If Apple’s AI stack hits OpenAI-like regulatory friction, domestic OEMs may gain a real window.

body.zh-CN:
**问题。** 我想更深入地看看，各大手机厂商究竟是如何真正把 AI 落地的：哪些能力运行在端侧，哪些依赖云端推理，模型由谁提供，以及哪些功能足够有黏性，足以影响用户的选机决策。

我目前的判断很简单：**苹果在中国大陆可能面临一个实质性的 AI 合规缺口**，而这个缺口可能会成为国内厂商的短期机会。

问题不只是苹果是否拥有强模型。在中国，AI 部署同时是一个产品问题、一个基础设施问题，以及一个监管问题。一个可用的手机 AI 技术栈需要：
- 合规的模型接入
- 本地化的云端与数据处理
- 安全过滤与可审计性
- 在助手、搜索、照片、消息、备忘录和生产力功能上的深度 OS 集成

这正是中国 OEM 可能在结构上更快的地方。华为、小米、OPPO、vivo 和荣耀可以把系统级集成、本地模型合作伙伴、国内云路径，以及针对中国应用生态和用户行为调优的功能结合起来。即便底层模型未必总是最强，**分发 + 合规 + 默认入口** 也可能比基准成绩更重要。

对苹果来说，这种挑战更像是结构性的。它最好的 AI 体验很可能依赖一套混合技术栈：端侧智能 + 云端支撑的辅助能力。在中国，任何面向云端的层都可能遇到与 OpenAI 式产品难以顺利落地同类的摩擦：审批、服务提供方约束、内容治理、数据本地化，以及合作复杂性。这并不意味着苹果没有竞争力，但它可能拖慢功能推出，或让整体体验变得碎片化。

**研究框架：**
- 模型来源：自研、本地合作伙伴，还是国外依赖
- 推理路径：端侧还是云端
- 功能覆盖面：助手、影像、搜索、笔记、通话、翻译
- 在中国的合规适配度

如果这个判断成立，那么移动 AI 的真正竞赛就不只是“谁拥有最聪明的模型”，而是 **谁能率先交付最连贯、最合规的默认体验。**
body.en:
**Question.** I want a deeper look at how major phone makers are actually landing AI: what runs on-device, what depends on cloud inference, who provides the model, and which features are sticky enough to influence device choice.

My current read is simple: **Apple may face a meaningful AI compliance gap in mainland China**, and that gap could become a short-term opportunity for domestic vendors.

The issue is not just whether Apple has a strong model. In China, AI deployment is simultaneously a product problem, an infrastructure problem, and a regulatory problem. A usable phone AI stack needs:
- compliant model access
- local cloud and data handling
- safety filtering and auditability
- deep OS integration across assistant, search, photos, messaging, notes, and productivity

This is where Chinese OEMs may be structurally faster. Huawei, Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo, and Honor can combine system-level integration with local model partners, domestic cloud paths, and features tuned for Chinese apps and behavior. Even if the underlying model is not always the strongest, **distribution + compliance + default placement** may matter more than benchmark quality.

For Apple, the challenge feels structural. Its best AI experience likely depends on a hybrid stack: on-device intelligence plus cloud-backed assistance. In China, any cloud-facing layer may run into the same class of friction that kept OpenAI-style products from landing cleanly: approvals, provider constraints, content governance, data locality, and partnership complexity. That does not make Apple non-competitive, but it could delay rollout or fragment the experience.

**Research frame:**
- model source: self-built, local partner, or foreign dependency
- inference path: on-device vs cloud
- feature surface: assistant, imaging, search, notes, call, translation
- compliance fit in China

If this holds, the real race in mobile AI is not just “who has the smartest model,” but **who can ship the most coherent compliant default experience first.**

title.zh-CN: 用签名事务令牌冻结执行上下文
title.en: Freezing Execution Context with a Signed Transaction Token

summary.zh-CN: 一个很实用的模式：从工具、上下文和设备信息中一次性解析出执行配置；对该配置签名；只把过期时间和身份信息放进短时效 JWT；然后在执行前验证配置没有发生漂移。
summary.en: A useful pattern: resolve execution config once from tool, context, and device; sign that config; put only expiry and identity into a short-lived JWT; then verify the config has not drifted before execution.

body.zh-CN:
我喜欢把 **事务创建** 视为执行意图被具体化的那一刻。

这个模式很简单：

- 创建事务时，根据 **工具**、**上下文** 和 **设备** 信息组装最终配置。
- 将其规范化成稳定结构。
- 加入盐值或一次性随机数，并对这份配置生成签名。
- 把 **过期时间戳** 放进 JWT。
- 返回该令牌，以及解析后的列表/输出和执行参数。

下一次调用时，在真正执行任何操作之前：

- 验证 JWT 有效且未过期
- 根据提交的配置重新计算签名
- 将其与原始签名值比对
- 如果有任何内容被篡改就拒绝执行

我喜欢这种模式的一点在于，它把第一步变成了一种 **提交点**。客户端可以持有令牌和参数，但之后无法悄悄改写执行上下文。当设备能力、工具选择或推导出的运行时参数会影响安全性、计费或可复现性时，这一点尤其重要。

有几个细节比乍看之下更重要：

- **规范序列化** 至关重要。如果配置没有被规范化，签名就会非常脆弱。
- 只对定义执行语义的字段签名；避免纳入噪声字段或瞬时值。
- 加入 **version** 字段，以便签名格式可以安全演进。
- 让 JWT 保持短时效。过期时间是安全模型的一部分，不只是清理工作。
- 平滑轮换签名密钥。

**核心思路：**把高成本的解析只做一次，对结果签名，并把后续执行视为验证，而不是重新解释。
body.en:
I like treating **transaction creation** as the moment where execution intent becomes concrete.

The shape is simple:

- When creating a transaction, assemble the final config from **tool**, **context**, and **device** information.
- Normalize it into a stable structure.
- Add a salt/nonce and generate a signature over that config.
- Put the **expiration timestamp** into a JWT.
- Return the token together with the resolved list/output and the execution parameters.

On the next call, before actually executing anything:

- verify the JWT is valid and unexpired
- recompute the signature from the submitted config
- compare it with the original signed value
- reject if anything was tampered with

What I like about this pattern is that it turns the first step into a kind of **commit point**. The client can hold the token and parameters, but it cannot quietly rewrite the execution context later. That matters when device capabilities, tool selection, or derived runtime args influence safety, billing, or reproducibility.

A few details matter more than they first appear:

- **Canonical serialization** is everything. If the config is not normalized, signing becomes fragile.
- Sign only the fields that define execution meaning; avoid noisy or ephemeral values.
- Include a **version** field so the signing format can evolve safely.
- Keep the JWT short-lived. Expiry is part of the security model, not just housekeeping.
- Rotate signing keys cleanly.

**The core idea:** do the expensive resolution once, sign the result, and treat later execution as verification rather than re-interpretation.

title.zh-CN: 尽早为多语言现实而设计
title.en: Design for Multilingual Reality Early

summary.zh-CN: 提醒自己：多语言支持应被视为系统设计问题，而不是上线前补做的一轮翻译。
summary.en: A reminder to treat multilingual support as a systems design problem, not a late translation pass.

body.zh-CN:
我反复重新学到的一点是：多语言支持不是翻译任务，它是一条设计约束。如果一个产品从一开始就默认只有一种语言，后面再补救时，代价往往比预期更丑陋。

有几件事，我现在把它们当作默认项，而不是边缘情况：

- **将内容与逻辑分离。** 不要把文案编码进控制流、数据库枚举值或分析标签里。
- **默认文本会变长也会变短。** 按钮、表格、导航标签和邮件模板都需要留出余量。
- **把 locale 看作不只是语言。** 日期、数字、排序、姓名、地址格式以及搜索行为都会随着 locale 改变。
- **用结构化方式处理复数和语法。** 仅靠字符串插值通常远远不够。
- **尊重书写系统。** UTF-8 全覆盖、字体覆盖率、输入法、换行规则以及从右到左布局都需要有意识地测试。
- **设计降级与回退。** 缺失翻译时，系统应当以优雅且可见的方式失败。
- **用“敌意样本”测试。** 超长的德语复合词、CJK 文本、阿拉伯语、带重音符号的搜索查询、混合语言内容。

更深一层的点在于架构：**语言会渗入整个系统**。URL 设计、slug、缓存、CMS 工作流、索引、可观测性以及客户支持，都会受到影响。由此可以得到一条很有用的纪律：内部使用稳定标识，外部进行本地化呈现。

我发现，思考多语言设计最便宜的时机，是第一条字符串进入 UI 之前。即使首个版本只发布单一语言，按多语言假设来构建，也会带来更清晰的边界和更少的隐藏依赖。它属于那种会悄悄改善整个系统的约束。
body.en:
One small note I keep relearning: multilingual support is not a translation task, it is a design constraint. If a product starts life assuming one language, the fixes later are always uglier than expected.

A few things I now treat as defaults, not edge cases:

- **Separate content from logic.** Never encode copy in control flow, database enums, or analytics labels.
- **Assume text expands and contracts.** Buttons, tables, nav labels, and email templates need slack.
- **Treat locale as more than language.** Dates, numbers, sorting, names, address formats, and search behavior all shift with locale.
- **Handle pluralization and grammar structurally.** String interpolation is rarely enough.
- **Respect scripts.** UTF-8 everywhere, font coverage, input methods, line breaking, and right-to-left layout need deliberate testing.
- **Design fallbacks.** Missing translations should fail gracefully and visibly.
- **Test with hostile samples.** Long German compounds, CJK text, Arabic, accented search queries, mixed-language content.

The deeper point is architectural: **language leaks into the whole system**. URL design, slugs, caching, CMS workflows, indexing, observability, and customer support all feel it. A useful discipline falls out of this: stable identifiers internally, localized presentation externally.

I have found that the cheapest time to think about multilingual design is before the first string lands in the UI. Even if the first release is single-language, building with multilingual assumptions produces cleaner boundaries and fewer hidden dependencies. It is one of those constraints that quietly improves the system.

title.zh-CN: 草图：一个让人工智能参与其中的微信同步层
title.en: Sketch: a WeChat sync layer with AI in the loop

summary.zh-CN: 我一直在反复琢磨一个想法：做一层由用户掌控的微信同步层——本地优先、可选云端支持；它的价值不在于自动化聊天，而在于把对话变成可搜索、可辅助调用的记忆。
summary.en: I keep circling around the idea of a user-controlled sync layer for WeChat: local-first, optionally cloud-backed, and useful not because it automates chat, but because it turns conversation into searchable, assistive memory.

body.zh-CN:
我总会回到同一个问题：有没有可能做出一个**微信同步工具**，把消息实时地，或在后台静默地，镜像到本地存储或私有云中——再用人工智能让这些对话变得更有用？

真正有意思的部分不是“人工智能代回复”，而是在聊天与记忆之间增加一层新的能力。

一个粗略的设计：

- **采集层** — 从用户可控的数据源摄取消息：桌面会话事件、通知，或用户明确导出的内容。我会强烈偏向于 **基于同意的采集**，而不是脆弱的抓取。
- **同步引擎** — 先把所有内容写入仅追加的本地日志，再按需复制到个人云。离线优先、加密、可回退。
- **人工智能层** — 语义搜索、摘要、翻译、回复草拟、主题提取、关系记忆。理想情况下，原始内容始终留在本地；只有在必要时，远程模型才会看到被尽量压缩的上下文。
- **界面层** — 不是替代现有聊天客户端，而是一个伴随式收件箱：可搜索、可批注，并能在恰当的时候呈现相关上下文。

它之所以吸引人，是因为消息应用如今承载着真实的工作和真实的关系，但它们依然是糟糕的知识系统。一个同步层可以把碎片化的聊天变成更接近个人基础设施的东西：可搜索的历史、跨语言沟通中介、轻量级客户关系管理，甚至是为持续协作提供持久记忆。

难点也很明显：平台政策、隐私、消息保真度、多设备协调，以及一个最根本的问题——后台同步能否以一种让人感到被尊重，而不是被抽取利用的方式被构建出来。

如果真要做，我的规则会很简单：**本地优先、同意优先、人工智能其次**。
body.en:
I keep returning to one question: could there be a **WeChat sync tool** that mirrors messages in real time, or quietly in the background, into a local store or private cloud — then uses AI to make those conversations more useful?

The interesting part is not “AI replies.” It is the idea of a new layer between chat and memory.

A rough design:

- **Capture layer** — ingest messages from a user-controlled source: desktop session events, notifications, or explicit exports. I would strongly prefer **consent-based collection** over brittle scraping.
- **Sync engine** — write everything to an append-only local log first, then optionally replicate to a personal cloud. Offline-first, encrypted, reversible.
- **AI layer** — semantic search, summaries, translation, reply drafting, topic extraction, relationship memory. Ideally raw content stays local; remote models only see minimized context when necessary.
- **Interface** — not a replacement chat client, but a companion inbox: searchable, annotatable, and able to surface context at the right moment.

What makes this compelling is that messaging apps are where real work and real relationships now live, but they are still poor knowledge systems. A sync layer could turn fragmented chat into something closer to personal infrastructure: searchable history, cross-language mediation, lightweight CRM, even a durable memory for ongoing collaborations.

The hard parts are obvious: platform policy, privacy, message fidelity, multi-device reconciliation, and the basic question of whether background sync can be built in a way that feels respectful rather than extractive.

If I were to build this, the rule would be simple: **local-first, consent-first, AI second**.

title.zh-CN: 用简单的 AIMD 规则实现自适应每日配额
title.en: Adaptive Daily Quotas with a Simple AIMD Rule

summary.zh-CN: 一个小巧的 AIMD 风格配额控制器：超额后次日减半，正常使用后逐步增加，并且支持按用户配置。
summary.en: A small AIMD-style quota controller: halve the next day after overuse, add gradually after normal use, and keep it configurable per user.

body.zh-CN:
我想让一个配额思路尽量保持简单：把每日上限做成自适应的，而不是固定值。它的形态本质上就是 **加性增加、乘性减少**，对于任何需要自我纠偏的机制来说，这通常都是个好信号。

**规则**
- 如果用户超出了今天的配额，明天的配额就变为 **当前值的 1/2**。
- 如果用户没有超出配额，明天的配额增加 **+n**。
- 始终将结果限制在配置好的下限和上限之间。

暂定上下限：
- 上限：**32 / 64**
- 下限：**4 / 8**

这些成对的数字只是不同分桶、套餐或操作类型的占位值。具体映射关系我现在还不需要完全定下来；但我确实需要系统支持这种能力。

我喜欢这种方案的地方：
- 突发峰值会被快速惩罚
- 恢复是渐进的
- 这种行为模式能向产品、客服和运维清楚解释
- 它避免了僵硬的一刀切配额模型

从第一天起，有两个约束我觉得不可妥协：
- **按用户配置。** 不同用户可能需要不同的初始配额、上限或步长。
- **后端可调。** 管理员应该能在不发版的情况下覆盖或微调配额。

所以，存储状态大概率应该包括：当前配额、最小配额、最大配额、增量 **n**、上次重置日期，以及一个带原因或审计轨迹的覆盖字段。

主要的调参问题是 **n**。太大，用户回到最大值的速度就会过快；太小，恢复过程又会显得惩罚性过强、没必要。但这个机制本身感觉很扎实：简单、可调，而且容易理解。
body.en:
One quota idea I want to keep simple: make the daily limit adaptive, not fixed. The shape is basically **additive increase, multiplicative decrease**, which is usually a good sign for anything that needs to self-correct.

**Rule**
- If a user exceeds today’s quota, tomorrow’s quota becomes **1/2 of the current value**.
- If a user stays within quota, tomorrow’s quota increases by **+n**.
- Always clamp the result to a configured floor and ceiling.

Tentative bounds:
- upper bound: **32 / 64**
- lower bound: **4 / 8**

The paired numbers are placeholders for different buckets, plans, or action types. I do not need that mapping fully decided yet; I do need the system to support it.

What I like about this approach:
- spikes are punished quickly
- recovery is gradual
- the behavior is explainable to product, support, and ops
- it avoids a rigid one-size-fits-all quota model

Two constraints feel non-negotiable from day one:
- **Per-user configuration.** Different users may need different starting quotas, caps, or step sizes.
- **Backend adjustability.** Admins should be able to override or tune quota without a deploy.

So the stored state should probably include: current quota, min quota, max quota, increment **n**, last reset date, and an override field with reason or audit trail.

The main tuning question is **n**. Too large, and users bounce back to max too fast. Too small, and recovery feels unnecessarily punitive. But the mechanism itself feels solid: simple, tunable, and easy to reason about.

title.zh-CN: GPT 作为长图算法的语义层
title.en: GPT as a Semantic Layer for Long-Image Algorithms

summary.zh-CN: GPT 也许无法提升长图处理流水线中像素级计算本身，但它可以显著改进围绕这条流水线的语义规则、评估标准和边界情况覆盖。
summary.en: GPT probably will not improve the pixel math of a long-image pipeline, but it can meaningfully improve the semantic rules, evaluation criteria, and edge-case coverage around it.

body.zh-CN:
我目前的判断是：GPT 不太可能优化长图系统的 **图像处理核心**。在拼接、缩放、接缝修复、去重或显著性估计这些任务上，它也很难胜过专用代码。真正的杠杆点在更高一层：**决策**。

对于长图——网页截图、聊天记录导出、文章卡片、海报式拼合图——最棘手的失败往往是语义层面的，而不是数值层面的。我们在意的是诸如自然的切分点、舒服的阅读节奏、完整的图注配对、不把人脸裁掉一半、不让标题悬空，以及在超高画布上保持合理的内容密度。这些目标人很容易描述，但很难直接编码。

一种实用模式是：
- 先提取结构：OCR、DOM、边界框、检测器输出
- 用 GPT 给区段打标、推断语义边界，并提出约束条件
- 把这些约束转成低成本的评分规则
- 让确定性代码完成真正的搜索、渲染和优化

GPT 最有帮助的地方：
- **失败分类**：给用户真正会注意到的坏情况命名并归类
- **测试生成**：构造多样且带对抗性的长页面示例
- **启发式设计**：把模糊的 UX 偏好翻译成可度量的惩罚项
- **调试**：解释为什么某个切分、裁剪或布局让人感觉不对劲

需要警惕的点也很明显：延迟、成本和非确定性。只要可能，我都会让模型避开热路径。让它改进评估器，而不是去画像素。可以离线用它打磨规则，或者在线时只让它处理结构化摘要，而不是原始的全分辨率图像。

所以，这个判断比“GPT 可以优化算法”要窄得多。更准确地说：它可以优化算法周围的 **规格定义**。对于长图产品来说，质量差距往往主要就存在于这里。
body.en:
My current take: GPT is unlikely to optimize the **image-processing core** of a long-image system. It will not beat specialized code at stitching, scaling, seam repair, de-duplication, or saliency estimation. The real leverage is one level higher: **decision-making**.

For long images—web page captures, chat exports, article cards, poster-like composites—the hardest failures are often semantic rather than numeric. We care about things like natural cut points, readable rhythm, intact figure-caption pairs, no half-cropped faces, no orphaned headers, and sensible density across a very tall canvas. Those goals are easy for humans to describe, but awkward to encode directly.

A practical pattern is:
- extract structure first: OCR, DOM, bounding boxes, detector outputs
- use GPT to label sections, infer semantic boundaries, and suggest constraints
- turn those constraints into cheap scoring rules
- let deterministic code do the actual search, rendering, and optimization

Where GPT helps most:
- **Failure taxonomy**: naming the bad cases users actually notice
- **Test generation**: creating diverse and adversarial long-page examples
- **Heuristic design**: translating vague UX taste into measurable penalties
- **Debugging**: explaining why a split, crop, or layout feels wrong

The caution is obvious: latency, cost, and nondeterminism. I would keep the model out of the hot path whenever possible. Let it improve the evaluator, not paint pixels. Use it offline to refine rules, or online only on structured summaries rather than raw full-resolution images.

So the claim is narrower than “GPT can optimize the algorithm.” More accurately: it can optimize the **specification** around the algorithm. For long-image products, that is often where most of the quality gap lives.

title.zh-CN: 长截图本质上是 OCR 的输入问题
title.en: Long screenshots are an OCR input problem

summary.zh-CN: 如果 iPhone 上的 OCR 在超长截图上表现很差，解决办法往往不是换一个更好的模型，而是换一个更好的输入：把图片切成更小、彼此重叠的裁剪块，再次在设备端做识别。
summary.en: If OCR on an iPhone performs badly on a very tall screenshot, the fix is often not a better model but a better input: slice the image into smaller overlapping crops and run on-device recognition again.

body.zh-CN:
超长截图对 iPhone 上的 OCR 来说，是一种很怪的失效场景。第一反应往往是把它发到服务器，用一个“更好”的模型。可在实际中，这通常并没有帮助。

出问题的似乎不只是识别器，还有图像几何本身。在一张长截图里，每一行文字都被塞进一块巨大的画布中，显得相对很小。等到 OCR 开始运行时，问题其实已经烙进像素里了：字形更小、间距更挤，版面启发式也会变差。后端模型无法可靠地还原那些已经不存在的细节。

我更信任的变通办法反而简单得多：**先把长截图切开，再在 iPhone 上重新跑一次 OCR**。

一些经验法则：
- 保持原始宽度。
- 沿纵向切成更小的裁剪块，每块大约 2–4 个屏幕高度。
- 在切片之间留一点重叠，避免边界附近的文字行丢失。
- 如果某一段特别密集，就再切得更小一些。

为什么这样有效：
- 每个裁剪块都能给识别器一个更接近正常页面的形状
- 文字在画面中的占比更高
- Live Text / 设备端 OCR 能在更好的局部上下文里再试一次
- 隐私和延迟都比走一趟服务器往返更好

所以我目前的结论是：这通常是个 **图像预处理** 问题，而不是“OCR 模型质量”问题。在动用服务器流水线之前，先试着把输入变得没那么反常。面对长截图，一个看起来很笨的切片步骤，往往就能胜过一个自称更聪明的 OCR 栈。
body.en:
Very tall screenshots are a weird failure mode for OCR on iPhone. The first instinct is to send them to a server and use a “better” model. In practice, that often does not help.

What seems to break is not only the recognizer but the image geometry. In a long screenshot, each line of text becomes relatively tiny inside one huge canvas. By the time OCR runs, the problem is already baked into the pixels: the glyphs are small, spacing is compressed, and layout heuristics get worse. A backend model cannot reliably recover detail that is no longer there.

The workaround I trust is much simpler: **slice the long screenshot, then run OCR on the iPhone again**.

A few rules of thumb:
- Keep the original width.
- Split vertically into smaller crops, roughly 2–4 screen heights each.
- Add a little overlap between slices so lines near boundaries are not lost.
- If one section is especially dense, cut it even smaller.

Why this works:
- each crop gives the recognizer a more normal page shape
- text occupies more of the frame
- Live Text / on-device OCR gets another chance with better local context
- privacy and latency stay better than a server round-trip

So my current takeaway is: this is often an **image preprocessing** problem, not an “OCR model quality” problem. Before reaching for a server pipeline, try making the input less pathological. For long screenshots, a dumb tiling step can beat a supposedly smarter OCR stack.

title.zh-CN: 事务级会话：一种轻量级防共享方案
title.en: Transaction-Scoped Sessions for Lightweight Anti-Sharing

summary.zh-CN: 一种轻量的认证模式：先校验与上一笔事务的连续性，再为当前事务签发短时会话，其余风险交由限流兜底。
summary.en: A small auth pattern: validate continuity from the previous transaction, issue a short-lived session for the current one, and rely on rate limits for the rest.

body.zh-CN:
**思路**
把一次用户操作视作一笔事务，哪怕它跨越了多个 HTTP 请求。客户端需要先证明自己与*上一笔*事务的连续性，服务器才会为当前事务签发一个新的会话。

**流程**
- 预检：客户端发送 `api_key`、上一个 `session_id`（首次运行时为空），以及作为弱设备上下文的 `hash(phone)`。
- 服务器在以下条件满足时放行：`api_key` 有效，且上一个 `session_id` 与该 `api_key` 最近一次存储的值一致。如果服务器端还没有存储值，也允许通过。
- 服务器签发 `new_session_id = hash(salt + api_key + timestamp)`。
- 存储两类数据：
  - 用于当前事务的 **运行时会话**，`runtime_ttl ≈ 10 min`（也可以更短，例如截图式操作用 60s）
  - 用于下一笔事务预检的 **上次会话记录**，`del_ttl ≈ 30 days`
- 事务内的每个请求都携带 `api_key + session_id`。
- 事务结束后，客户端丢弃该会话，服务器删除运行时记录。

**故障处理**
如果客户端在事务中途挂掉，就让 `runtime_ttl` 或最大调用次数上限使其自然过期。如果状态损坏，就把服务器端存储的会话值重置为空，并允许下一次预检重新进入这一循环。

**这能带来什么**
**这增加的是阻力，不是安全性。** 它增强了连续性，也增加了一些防共享阻力，但并不是强设备绑定。有意规避的人依然可以维持会话存活并转售访问权限，所以 **每日限流仍然是必需的**。另外，如果客户端把上一个会话存到本地文件或 iCloud 中，就要明确：多设备同步到底是特性，还是漏洞。
body.en:
**Idea**
Treat one user operation as a transaction, even if it spans several HTTP requests. The client proves continuity from the *previous* transaction before the server issues a fresh session for the current one.

**Flow**
- Preflight: client sends `api_key`, previous `session_id` (empty on first run), and `hash(phone)` as soft device context.
- Server allows if `api_key` is valid and the previous `session_id` matches the last stored value for that key. If the server has no stored value yet, allow.
- Server mints `new_session_id = hash(salt + api_key + timestamp)`.
- Store two things:
  - **runtime session** for the current transaction, `runtime_ttl ≈ 10 min` (or much shorter, e.g. 60s for screenshot-style operations)
  - **last-session record** for prechecking the next transaction, `del_ttl ≈ 30 days`
- Every in-transaction request carries `api_key + session_id`.
- When the transaction ends, the client discards the session and the server deletes the runtime record.

**Failure handling**
If the client dies mid-transaction, let `runtime_ttl` or a max-call cap expire it naturally. If state gets corrupted, reset the server-side stored session to empty and allow the next preflight to re-enter the cycle.

**What this buys me**
**This is friction, not security.** It adds continuity and some anti-sharing resistance, but it is not strong device binding. A determined actor can still keep a session alive and resell access, so **daily rate limits are still necessary**. Also, if the client stores the previous session in a local file or iCloud, decide explicitly whether multi-device sync is a feature or a bug.

title.zh-CN: 视频 -> GIF：稳定性优先的兼容层
title.en: Video -> GIF as a Stability-First Compatibility Layer

summary.zh-CN: 我把兼容性处理管线从直接处理视频切换为生成 GIF，因为它在高负载下稳定得多；下一步是在不丢掉这些运维收益的前提下，有选择地把部分画质补回来。
summary.en: I switched the compatibility pipeline from direct video handling to GIF generation because it is dramatically more stable under load; the next step is to selectively buy back quality without losing those operational gains.

body.zh-CN:
**发生了什么变化**

我完成了媒体处理管线中第一个真正有意义的优化：把兼容路径从直接处理视频改成 **视频 -> GIF**。

这更多不是为了美观，而是为了活下来。单个视频任务就很容易把内存占用推到 **5 GB** 以上。GIF 路径的表现要好得多：内存保持稳定，CPU 使用率更低，处理速度也明显更快。实际效果是，对于资源受限的机器和突发式负载，GIF 是安全得多的默认选项。

缺点也很明确：**GIF 会丢失细节**。肉眼可见的失真是存在的，所以这是一笔以稳定性优先的取舍，而不是画质上的胜利。

**我下一步想改进什么**

- **关键帧替换**：在生成 GIF 之后，尽量补回一部分分辨率和主观清晰度。
- **I-frame + 少量 P-frames**：作为折中方案。它应该能比单纯的 GIF 保留更多细节，但大概率依赖于上传完整的源视频。
- **网络感知自适应**：根据当前网络状况来决定 GIF 尺寸、是否上传原始视频以及传输速率，而不是套用一套固定策略。
- **多通道匹配**：当前的匹配器还是单通道，扩展后也许能提升鲁棒性。
- **直接从 ffmpeg 流式处理**：让解码和转换以增量方式进行，从而避免再次出现内存暴涨。

**产品约束**

如果这真的做成一项服务，我大概率需要三层限制：

- 每日配额
- 每小时配额
- 分钟级突发控制

定价看起来也很直接：

- **订阅制**：包含固定额度
- **按量计费**：用于超额或更重度的用户

模式已经越来越清晰：先把 GIF 作为安全基线，只在画质确实重要的地方再投入复杂度。
body.en:
**What changed**

I finished the first meaningful optimization in the media pipeline: moving the compatibility path from direct video handling to **video -> GIF**.

This was less about aesthetics and more about survival. A single video job can easily push memory usage past **5 GB**. The GIF path behaves much better: memory stays stable, CPU usage is lower, and processing is noticeably faster. In practice, that makes GIF a much safer default for constrained machines and bursty workloads.

The downside is real: **GIF loses detail**. There is visible distortion, so this is a stability-first trade, not a quality win.

**What I want to improve next**

- **Keyframe replacement** to recover some resolution and perceived sharpness after GIF generation.
- **I-frame + a small number of P-frames** as a middle ground. That should preserve more detail than GIF alone, but it likely depends on having the full source video uploaded.
- **Network-aware adaptation**: choose GIF dimensions, whether to upload the original video, and transfer rate based on current network conditions instead of using one fixed policy.
- **Multi-channel matching**: the current matcher is single-channel; expanding it may improve robustness.
- **Stream directly from ffmpeg** so decoding and transformation happen incrementally, which should avoid another memory blow-up.

**Product guardrails**

If this becomes a real service, I probably need limits at three levels:

- daily quota
- hourly quota
- minute-level burst control

Pricing also seems straightforward:

- **subscription** with a fixed allowance
- **usage-based billing** for overflow or heavier users

The pattern is becoming clear: use GIF as the safe baseline, then spend complexity only where quality actually matters.

title.zh-CN: 剖析视频流水线中单次请求的成本
title.en: Profiling the Single-Request Cost in a Video Pipeline

summary.zh-CN: 测试过程中暴露出一个很有价值的失效模式：单个视频请求就能吃满一个 CPU 核心，而且内存会随着输入大小增长。下一步应该把成本拆分到 ffmpeg 和算法两侧，先找出真正的瓶颈，再去优化，而不是靠猜。
summary.en: A useful failure mode surfaced during testing: one video request can saturate a single CPU core, and memory grows with input size. The right next step is to split the cost between ffmpeg and the algorithm, then optimize the real bottleneck instead of guessing.

body.zh-CN:
在测试视频流水线时，我注意到一个简单但重要的失效模式：**单个请求就能完全占满一个 CPU 核心**，并且**内存使用会随着视频大小上升**。

这个观察很重要，因为它排除了一个常见借口。这不只是并发问题。即使在请求数 = 1 的情况下，这条流水线本身也已经很昂贵。换句话说，基础成本太高了。

眼下最直接的问题是，压力究竟主要来自哪里：**ffmpeg** 还是 **算法**。

如果热点在 ffmpeg，优化路径主要就是偏系统层面的工作：
- 避免不必要的转码或格式转换
- 尽量以流式方式处理数据，而不是在内存里缓冲过多内容
- 检查 decode / resize 步骤是否产生了额外拷贝
- 调整调用参数，而不是把 ffmpeg 当成黑盒

如果热点在算法，关注点就要转移：
- 减少需要处理的帧数
- 更早进行降采样
- 缩小中间 tensor 或 buffer 的规模
- 收紧内存生命周期，并尽可能复用分配结果

所以下一步不是“优化整条流水线”，而是在流程中放一个清晰的断点，分别测量每个阶段的成本。**先找出主导成本，再有针对性地优化。**

有两条护栏值得立刻加上，即使在深入调优之前也是如此：
- **限制上传文件大小**
- **在分析前先对输入视频做降采样**

这对视频系统来说算是一个很好的提醒：在讨论规模化之前，先确保单次请求路径是健康的。如果一个请求就已经能吃满一个核心，并且内存会随着输入大小不断增长，那系统架构其实已经在告诉你该从哪里下手。
body.en:
While testing the video pipeline, I noticed a simple but important failure mode: **a single request can fully occupy one CPU core**, and **memory usage rises with video size**.

That observation matters because it rules out a common excuse. This is not just a concurrency problem. Even at request count = 1, the pipeline is already expensive. In other words, the baseline cost is too high.

The immediate question is where the pressure actually comes from: **ffmpeg** or **the algorithm**.

If the hotspot is ffmpeg, the optimization path is mostly systems work:
- avoid unnecessary transcoding or format conversion
- stream data instead of buffering too much in memory
- check whether decode / resize steps are creating extra copies
- tune invocation parameters rather than treating ffmpeg as a black box

If the hotspot is the algorithm, then the focus shifts:
- reduce the number of frames processed
- downsample earlier
- shrink intermediate tensors or buffers
- tighten memory lifecycle and reuse allocations where possible

So the next step is not “optimize the whole pipeline.” It is to put a clean breakpoint in the flow and measure each stage separately. **Find the dominant cost first, then optimize with intent.**

Two guardrails are worth adding immediately, even before deep tuning:
- **limit upload file size**
- **downsample input video before analysis**

This feels like a good reminder for video systems in general: before discussing scale, make sure the single-request path is sane. If one request can already pin a core and grow memory with input size, the architecture is telling you where to look.

title.zh-CN: 现代后端开发的核心组件选择
title.en: Core Component Choices for Modern Backend Development

summary.zh-CN: 一份简明指南，涵盖构建可扩展高效后端服务的关键库与工具，包括用于关系数据库的 SQLModel、Redis 客户端、FastAPI、AI 集成、语言检测、标点修正、Markdown 处理及分布式限流解决方案。
summary.en: A concise guide to key libraries and tools forming the backbone of scalable, efficient backend services, including SQLModel for RDS, Redis clients, FastAPI, AI integration, language detection, punctuation correction, markdown processing, and distributed rate limiting.

body.zh-CN:
在当今构建后端系统时，组件的选型决定了开发效率、可维护性以及系统性能。以下是我精心挑选并长期实践的核心工具清单：

- **关系数据库访问：SQLModel**
  依赖 SQLAlchemy 和 Pydantic，[SQLModel](https://github.com/tiangolo/sqlmodel) 提供简洁且支持类型提示的 Python ORM，降低了操作关系型数据库的样板代码负担。

- **Redis 客户端**
  结合官方的 [redis-py](https://github.com/redis/redis-py) 和高性能的 `hiredis` 解析器，实现高效的缓存和消息队列支持，适合低延迟场景。

- **Web 框架：FastAPI**
  现代异步优先并具备强类型系统的 FastAPI，是构建 RESTful API 的首选，兼顾快速开发与生产级稳定性。

- **AI 集成**
  通过接入 ZhipuAI 与 MoonShot，扩展自然语言处理能力，无需重复造轮子即可实现复杂语言任务。

- **语言检测与标点处理**
  - 使用 `langdetect` 做语言快速识别。
  - 中文方面依赖 PaddleHub 的 `auto_punc` 模型自动补全标点。
  - 英文标点则基于 NLTK 的 Punkt 标注。

- **Markdown 与文档转换**
  利用 `pydandoc` 和 `biplist` 流畅地实现 Markdown 到 HTML 的转换，支持丰富文本操作流程。

- **分布式限流**
  采用 [PyrateLimiter](https://github.com/vutran1710/PyrateLimiter) 作为分布式限流工具，避免中心化压力，保障横向扩展时 API 的稳定性。

这套工具链兼顾技术先进性与开发者体验，是 2024 年构建响应式且可维护后端服务的实用方案。

---

### 参考链接

- SQLModel: https://github.com/tiangolo/sqlmodel
- redis-py & hiredis: https://github.com/redis/redis-py
- PyrateLimiter: https://github.com/vutran1710/PyrateLimiter
body.en:
When architecting backend systems today, component choices critically shape development speed, maintainability, and performance. Here's my distilled stack of curated tools and libraries that consistently deliver.

- **RDS Access with SQLModel**: Built on top of SQLAlchemy and Pydantic, [SQLModel](https://github.com/tiangolo/sqlmodel) offers a clean, Pythonic ORM with type hints, easing relational database interactions without boilerplate.

- **Redis Clients**: Leveraging [redis-py](https://github.com/redis/redis-py) combined with the ultra-fast `hiredis` parser ensures efficient caching and messaging. The synergy here is key for latency-sensitive workloads.

- **Web Framework: FastAPI**: Modern, async-first, and type-driven, FastAPI remains my go-to for RESTful APIs, blending rapid development with production-grade robustness.

- **AI Integration**: Incorporating ZhipuAI and MoonShot for advanced language processing enriches capabilities without reinventing core ML functionality.

- **Language & Punctuation Tools**:
  - Language detection powered by `langdetect` for quick locale guesses.
  - Chinese text enhancement through PaddleHub's models (`auto_punc` for auto punctuation).
  - English punctuation tokenization anchored in NLTK’s Punkt.

- **Markdown & Document Conversion**: `pydandoc` along with `biplist` enables converting markdown to HTML seamlessly, facilitating rich text workflows.

- **Distributed Rate Limiting**: To safeguard APIs from overuse without centralized bottlenecks, [PyrateLimiter](https://github.com/vutran1710/PyrateLimiter) provides flexible distributed rate controls, vital for horizontal scaling.

This curated ecosystem reflects a balance between cutting-edge performance and developer ergonomics. It’s a practical toolkit for engineers aiming to build responsive, maintainable backends in 2024.

---

### References

- SQLModel: https://github.com/tiangolo/sqlmodel
- redis-py & hiredis: https://github.com/redis/redis-py
- PyrateLimiter: https://github.com/vutran1710/PyrateLimiter

title.zh-CN: 高效工作与沟通的核心原则
title.en: Principles for Effective Work and Communication

summary.zh-CN: 一个简洁框架，指引如何打造突破性产品，确保清晰负责的沟通，聚焦结果、为用户提供简洁体验，并体现责任归属。
summary.en: A concise framework for creating breakthrough products and ensuring clear, responsible communication by focusing on outcomes, simplicity for users, and ownership accountability.

body.zh-CN:
### 创造理论可行但尚不存在的产品
专注于发掘并构建尚未出现但理论上可行的创新产品，打开新的增长空间。这种前瞻性思维鼓励超越渐进式改进的突破。

### 以结果为导向的外部视角
以结果衡量成功，而非过程中的付出多少。优先选择最明智、高效的方法，强调效果胜过忙碌。

### 为用户简洁优雅地隐藏复杂性
提供最简单的体验，巧妙隐藏专业领域和系统背后的复杂性，让用户能够轻松使用。

### 严谨、清晰且逻辑完整的表达
坚持表达准确、逻辑充分，避免模糊不清的语言，不转述似是而非的结论，有节奏感地解决问题。追求极致的同时，不要藏着掖着解决方案。

### 首问负责制
默认自己是项目或问题的 Owner，主动提出解决方案，持续跟进直至彻底解决，并及时反馈，推动责任落实，避免问题拖延。

---

整合这些原则，团队能够激发创新潜力，同时保持严谨负责的沟通方式，推动持续且高效的成果落地。
body.en:
### Creating Theoretically Feasible, Yet Non-Existent Products
Focus on opening new growth spaces by imagining and building products that don’t exist yet but are theoretically viable. This forward-looking mindset encourages innovation beyond incremental improvements.

### Outcome-Centric, External Perspective
Judge success by results rather than effort or process. Prioritize choosing the smartest approach even if it appears less labor-intensive, emphasizing effectiveness over busyness.

### Simplifying Complexity for the User
Deliver the simplest possible experience by elegantly hiding complexity—both from the professional domain and the underlying system—enabling users to engage effortlessly.

### Rigorous, Clear, and Logical Expression
Communicate with precision and logical completeness. Avoid ambiguous language, refrain from echoing dubious conclusions, and maintain a rhythmic approach to problem-solving. Strive for excellence but avoid holding back solutions.

### The First-Question Ownership Principle
Assume ownership by default: take initiative to propose solutions, follow through until resolution, and provide timely feedback. This fosters accountability and ensures issues don’t linger unresolved.

---

By integrating these principles, teams can unlock innovation potential while maintaining disciplined and responsible communication—driving consistent, impactful progress.

title.zh-CN: 苹果式 AI：起点是输入，不是提示词
title.en: Apple-Style AI Starts With Inputs, Not Prompts

summary.zh-CN: 一份关于苹果式 AI 层的产品草图：把用户现有输入当作界面，并围绕短路径、上下文驱动的操作进行优化。
summary.en: A product sketch for an Apple-like AI layer that treats existing user inputs as the interface and optimizes for shallow, contextual actions.

body.zh-CN:
苹果式 AI 应该从输入开始，而不是提示词。真正的交互面，就是用户手头已经有的内容：照片、截图、长图、由视频帧转出的文本、语音转文字、剪贴板内容、链接、PDF、文档，以及纯文本文件。

核心交互规则很简单：缩短操作路径。跳过不必要的说明，上手引导只展示一次，并让系统从最近的操作中推断意图。如果我刚复制了一个链接，下一步大概率就是摘要或翻译。如果我截了一张聊天截图，下一步多半是提取内容、解释含义，或起草回复。AI 应该像一个基于上下文的动作选择器，而不是一个空白聊天框。

**能力层级**
- **通用助手**：联网、开放式问答，以简短多轮会话为主。
- **搜索助手**：深度搜索、综合整理与引用；默认单轮。
- **阅读 / 写作助手**：提取、总结、润色、头脑风暴、点评，然后保存到备忘录。
- **翻译器**：识别源语言，并翻译成用户偏好的目标语言。
- **聊天助手**：总结最近 N 条消息，生成候选回复，改写语气，并润色别扭表达。
- **记忆层**：偏好、长期记忆，以及一个覆盖备忘录、语音备忘录和任务的“备忘录问答”RAG。

一个有用的约束是：不要在本地工具调用上过度投入。快捷指令已经覆盖了其中很多能力。AI 的价值，更应该花在理解、综合与路径压缩上。

**配套工具**
长截图拼接、带上下文的快速灵感记录，以及轻量提醒，会让这层 AI 更具黏性。

**运营现实**
后端主导的鉴权、区域定价、配额、速率限制、上传上限和行为分析并不光鲜，但它们本就是产品的一部分。
body.en:
Apple-style AI should start from inputs, not prompts. The real surface area is whatever the user already has in hand: photos, screenshots, long images or video frames converted to text, voice turned into text, clipboard content, links, PDFs, docs, and plain text files.

The main UX rule is simple: reduce path depth. Skip unnecessary explanation, show onboarding once, and let intent be inferred from recent actions. If I just copied a link, the likely next step is summarize or translate. If I captured a chat screenshot, the next step is probably extract, explain, or draft a reply. AI should feel like a contextual verb picker, not a blank chat box.

**Capability layers**
- **General assistant**: web-connected, free-form Q&A, with short multi-turn sessions.
- **Search assistant**: deep search, synthesis, and citations; single-turn by default.
- **Reading / writing assistant**: extract, summarize, polish, brainstorm, critique, then save to Notes.
- **Translator**: detect source language and translate into the user’s preferred target language.
- **Chat helper**: summarize the last N messages, generate reply candidates, rewrite tone, and smooth awkward phrasing.
- **Memory layer**: preferences, long-term memory, and an “Ask Memo” RAG over notes, voice memos, and tasks.

A useful constraint: do not overinvest in local tool calling. Shortcuts already covers a lot of that. The AI value is better spent on interpretation, synthesis, and path compression.

**Supporting utilities**
Long screenshot stitching, quick idea capture with context, and lightweight reminders make the AI layer stickier.

**Operational realities**
Backend-centric auth, regional pricing, quotas, rate limits, upload caps, and behavior analytics are not glamorous, but they are part of the product.

title.zh-CN: 利用Git进行自动化多分支部署
title.en: Automating Multi-Branch Deployment Using Git

summary.zh-CN: 本文介绍了利用Git裸仓库和钩子实现自动化多分支部署的实用方案。包括创建专用Git用户、在服务器上初始化裸仓库、配置支持分支创建、更新和删除的post-update钩子脚本，以及本地Git配置实现自动化推送部署。
summary.en: This article details a practical setup for automating multi-branch deployments using Git bare repositories and hooks. It covers creating a dedicated Git user, initializing a bare repository on the server, configuring a versatile post-update hook script to handle branch creation, updates, and deletions, and local Git configurations to enable streamlined automatic deployment.

body.zh-CN:
## 概述

通过Git推送触发自动部署，大幅简化多分支开发中的持续集成流程。本文介绍了利用Linux服务器上的Git裸仓库和钩子脚本，实现多分支自动化部署的简洁方案。

---

## 1. 创建专用Git用户

出于安全和隔离考虑，创建一个仅限Git操作的系统用户：

```bash
useradd git
passwd git
# 修改登录Shell为git-shell，防止普通登录
vi /etc/passwd
# 将git用户的shell改为：
git:x:1000:1000::/home/git:/usr/bin/git-shell
```

这样可禁止该用户登录服务器，只能执行Git命令。

---

## 2. 在服务器初始化裸仓库

裸仓库作为中央代码库接收所有推送。使用如下命令创建：

```bash
git init --bare /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
cd /opt/webapps/project-bare.git/hooks
cp post-update.sample post-update
vi post-update
```

---

## 3. 编写支持多分支的`post-update`钩子脚本

`post-update`在每次推送后触发，用于更新工作区对应分支代码。以下为优化后的脚本内容：

```sh
#!/bin/sh

unset GIT_DIR
WORK_DIR="/opt/webapps/project"
mkdir -p ${WORK_DIR}
cd ${WORK_DIR} || exit 1

# 获取当前分支与推送目标分支
CURR_BRANCH=$(git symbolic-ref --short -q HEAD)
TARGET_REF="$1"
TARGET_BRANCH=$(echo "${TARGET_REF}" | awk -F/ '{print $3}')

echo "当前分支: ${CURR_BRANCH}"
echo "目标分支: ${TARGET_BRANCH}"

# 若未初始化仓库则初始化
if [ ! -d .git ]; then
  git init
  git remote add origin /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
fi

# 重置工作区，清理未跟踪文件
 git reset --hard
 git clean -df

# 拉取裸仓库所有分支最新代码
 git fetch origin

# 处理分支删除情形
if [ ! -f "/opt/webapps/project-bare.git/refs/heads/${TARGET_BRANCH}" ]; then
  echo "远程分支 ${TARGET_BRANCH} 已删除。"
  if [ "${TARGET_BRANCH}" = "${CURR_BRANCH}" ]; then
    git checkout master
  fi
  git branch -D "${TARGET_BRANCH}" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
  if [ "${TARGET_BRANCH}" = "${CURR_BRANCH}" ]; then
    echo "当前分支更新，执行rebase。"
    git rebase origin/${TARGET_BRANCH}
  else
    echo "切换至分支 ${TARGET_BRANCH}"
    git branch -D "${TARGET_BRANCH}" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    git checkout -b "${TARGET_BRANCH}" origin/${TARGET_BRANCH}
  fi
fi

echo "代码更新完成。"

# 业务部署流程（根据项目实际调整）
npm install
rm -rf dist
npm run build
pm2 restart project

echo "部署成功完成。"
```

---

## 4. 配置权限

确保Git用户拥有仓库及工作目录权限：

```bash
chown -R git:git /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
mkdir -p /opt/webapps/project
chown -R git:git /opt/webapps/project
```

---

## 5. 本地仓库配置自动部署远程

加入远程仓库，推送代码触发自动部署：

```bash
git remote add deployment git@www.huhaoyu.com:/opt/webapps/project-bare.git
git push deployment master
```

推送到`deployment`远程时即执行服务器端`post-update`钩子。

---

## 总结

| 步骤 | 目的                               |
|------|----------------------------------|
| 1    | 创建受限Shell的Git用户           |
| 2    | 初始化裸仓库                     |
| 3    | 配置支持多分支的`post-update`脚本 |
| 4    | 设置仓库及工作目录权限           |
| 5    | 本地配置远程推送实现自动部署     |

---

该方案确保部署目录实时更新对应分支代码，并自动处理分支新增、更新与删除。根据项目实际需求调整部署命令，实现高效自动化分支管理与发布。
body.en:
## Overview

Automated deployment triggered by Git pushes streamlines continuous integration workflows, especially when dealing with multiple active branches. This guide reveals a straightforward setup to support multi-branch deployment via a bare Git repository on a Linux server, using shell scripts within Git hooks.

---

## 1. Create a Dedicated Git User for Deployment

For security and isolation, create a system user with restricted shell access:

```bash
useradd git
passwd git
# Restrict to git-shell to prevent arbitrary login
vi /etc/passwd
# Change git's login shell to /usr/bin/git-shell:
git:x:1000:1000::/home/git:/usr/bin/git-shell
```

This prevents direct shell login and confines the user to Git command execution only.

---

## 2. Initialize a Bare Git Repository on Server

A bare repository acts as the centralized source for all incoming pushes. Initialize it as follows:

```bash
git init --bare /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
cd /opt/webapps/project-bare.git/hooks
cp post-update.sample post-update
vi post-update
```

---

## 3. Implement Multi-Branch Deployment in `post-update` Hook

The `post-update` hook triggers after every push to update the working deployment directory according to the branch pushed. Below is a refined shell script:

```sh
#!/bin/sh

unset GIT_DIR
WORK_DIR="/opt/webapps/project"
mkdir -p ${WORK_DIR}
cd ${WORK_DIR} || exit 1

# Identify current and target branches
CURR_BRANCH=$(git symbolic-ref --short -q HEAD)
TARGET_REF="$1"
TARGET_BRANCH=$(echo "${TARGET_REF}" | awk -F/ '{print $3}')

echo "Current branch: ${CURR_BRANCH}"
echo "Target branch: ${TARGET_BRANCH}"

# Initialize repo if not exists
if [ ! -d .git ]; then
git init
# Add remote pointing to the bare repo
 git remote add origin /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
fi

# Reset and clean to avoid conflicts
 git reset --hard
 git clean -df

# Fetch latest from origin
 git fetch origin

# Handle deleted branches (remote ref missing)
if [ ! -f "/opt/webapps/project-bare.git/refs/heads/${TARGET_BRANCH}" ]; then
 echo "Branch ${TARGET_BRANCH} deleted upstream."
 if [ "${TARGET_BRANCH}" = "${CURR_BRANCH}" ]; then
   git checkout master
 fi
 git branch -D "${TARGET_BRANCH}" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
 if [ "${TARGET_BRANCH}" = "${CURR_BRANCH}" ]; then
   echo "On updated branch ${TARGET_BRANCH}, rebasing..."
   git rebase origin/${TARGET_BRANCH}
 else
   echo "Switching to branch ${TARGET_BRANCH}"
   git branch -D "${TARGET_BRANCH}" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
   git checkout -b "${TARGET_BRANCH}" origin/${TARGET_BRANCH}
 fi
fi

echo "Git pull and sync complete."

# Deployment steps — adjust as per your app's build process
npm install
rm -rf dist
npm run build
pm2 restart project

echo "Deployment completed successfully."
```

---

## 4. Setup Permissions

Make sure the Git user owns the repository and deployment directory:

```bash
chown -R git:git /opt/webapps/project-bare.git
mkdir -p /opt/webapps/project
chown -R git:git /opt/webapps/project
```

---

## 5. Local Git Remote Configuration for Auto-Deploy

Configure your local repository to push to the deployment remote repository:

```bash
git remote add deployment git@www.huhaoyu.com:/opt/webapps/project-bare.git
git push deployment master
```

Pushing to `deployment` triggers the `post-update` hook, initiating automatic deployment.

---

## Summary

| Step | Purpose                                  |
|-------|-----------------------------------------|
| 1     | Create Git user with restricted shell  |
| 2     | Initialize bare repository              |
| 3     | Configure `post-update` for multi-branch deployment |
| 4     | Set directory ownership and permissions |
| 5     | Push code remotely to trigger deployment |

---

This approach ensures your deployment directory reflects the latest pushed code per branch, handling branch updates and deletions automatically. Modify the deployment steps (`npm install`, build, restart) according to your application's lifecycle.

title.zh-CN: 聚类算法概述（k-Means++、FCM、凝聚层次聚类和DBSCAN）
title.en: Overview of Clustering Algorithms (k-Means++, FCM, Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering, DBSCAN)

summary.zh-CN: 本文回顾了经典聚类算法——k-Means++（k-means优化初始化）、模糊C均值（FCM）、凝聚层次聚类和DBSCAN，介绍了它们的核心概念、算法流程、优缺点。通过Python代码示例和示意图，帮助读者理解基础知识，并链接到一个现代的多语言聚类教程仓库，方便实操学习。
summary.en: This article revisits classic clustering algorithms—k-Means++ (a refined k-means initializer), Fuzzy C-Means (FCM), Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering, and DBSCAN—outlining their core concepts, algorithmic workflows, advantages, and limitations. Illustrated with Python code excerpts and diagrams, it provides foundational insights and connects to a modern, multi-language clustering playbook repository for hands-on exploration.

body.zh-CN:
## 聚类简介
聚类是无监督的学习方法，旨在将数据划分为若干簇，使得簇内相似度高，簇间相似度低。广泛应用于数据挖掘和知识发现。

### 核心概念
- **层次与划分式聚类：** 层次聚类形成一个嵌套的树结构，而划分式聚类将数据分配到固定数量且互斥的簇中。
- **互斥、重叠与模糊聚类：** 点可以被严格分配到单个簇（硬聚类），也可以按隶属度分配到多个簇（模糊聚类）。隶属度之和为1。
- **完全聚类与部分聚类：** 完全聚类要求所有数据点都分配到簇，部分聚类允许存在噪声点不属于任何簇。

### 簇的类型
- **明显分离型：** 簇间任意点距离大于簇内任意点距离。
- **基于原型：** 点距离本簇中心比其他簇中心更近。
- **基于图：** 点作为节点，边权表示相似度，通过图结构划分簇。
- **基于密度：** 簇为密度相连区域，适用于形状不规则且含噪声的数据。

## 常用聚类算法分类对比
| 算法               | 方法类别         | 是否需要预先指定k | 是否能识别噪声 | 是否适合任意形状 | 典型复杂度          | 适用场景                   |
|--------------------|------------------|------------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------------|----------------------------|
| **k-Means++**       | 划分式原型       | 是               | 否             | 通常不适用      | 时间 O(nkt)，空间 O(n+k) | 球形、分离明显的簇           |
| **模糊C均值(FCM)** | 软划分           | 是               | 否             | 通常不适用      | 时间 O(nkt)，空间 O(nk)    | 存在簇间重叠，需保留隶属度   |
| **凝聚层次聚类**   | 层次聚类         | 合并时不需要       | 有限           | 某些情形可用    | 时间 O(n^3)，空间 O(n^2)   | 小规模数据，关注层次结构     |
| **DBSCAN**          | 基于密度         | 否               | 是             | 是              | 时间 O(n^2)，空间 O(n^2)    | 不规则簇，存在噪声           |

## 经典算法介绍

### 1. k-Means++ (硬划分k均值聚类)
k-Means++是k-means的改良版，通过优化初始中心选择减少陷入局部最优的风险。

**算法步骤：**
1. 随机选取第一个聚类中心。
2. 后续中心根据距离现有中心的远近，以距离平方为权重概率选取。
3. 将所有点分配给最近中心。
4. 计算每个簇的新中心（均值）。
5. 重复3-4步骤，直至中心移动小于阈值。

**优点：** 简单、快速、效果可解释，适合球形簇。

**缺点：** 需先决簇数；对噪声敏感；不适用于复杂形状簇。

**核心代码示例：**
```python
import random
import math

def dist_sq(p1, p2):
    return (p1[0]-p2[0])**2+(p1[1]-p2[1])**2

def kmeans_plus_plus_init(points, k):
    centers = [random.choice(points)]
    for _ in range(1,k):
        dists = [min(dist_sq(p,c) for c in centers) for p in points]
        total = sum(dists)
        probs = [d/total for d in dists]
        r = random.random()
        cum_prob = 0
        for p, prob in zip(points, probs):
            cum_prob += prob
            if cum_prob >= r:
                centers.append(p)
                break
    return centers
```

### 2. 模糊C均值(FCM)
FCM允许样本对多个簇具有不同程度的隶属度，隶属度满足在各簇之和为1，由一个指数m控制模糊度。

**主要步骤：**
- 初始化聚类中心(可用k-means++初始方式)。
- 计算点对中心的隶属度：

  \[ u_{i,j} = \left(\sum_{k=1}^c \left(\frac{d(x_j,v_i)}{d(x_j,v_k)}\right)^{\frac{2}{m-1}}\right)^{-1} \]

- 更新聚类中心：

  \[ v_i = \frac{\sum_{j=1}^n u_{i,j}^m x_j}{\sum_{j=1}^n u_{i,j}^m} \]

- 迭代直至聚类中心收敛。

**特点：** 保留软隶属度，计算复杂度较高。仍需指定簇数，无法识别噪声。

### 3. 凝聚层次聚类
每个数据点初始为单独的簇，反复合并距离最小的簇直到满足簇数限制或合并比例阈值。

**优点：** 可观察多层次结构，无需预设k；对噪声有限容忍。

**缺点：** 计算和存储开销大；合并是不可逆操作，可能过度合并。

### 4. DBSCAN
基于密度连接的聚类算法，参数包括邻域半径`Eps`和最小邻居点数`MinPts`。

- 核心点：邻域内点数≥`MinPts`。
- 边界点：邻域点数不足，但邻域内存在核心点。
- 噪声点：既非核心点也非边界点，保持孤立。

**聚类流程：**
- 密度可达的所有核心点组成同一簇。
- 边界点归属邻域内的核心点簇。
- 噪声点不归入任何簇。

**优点：** 适合任意形状簇，自动识别噪声。

**缺点：** 参数较敏感；计算复杂度相对较高。

## 参数影响总结
| 算法        | 关键参数                   | 参数变大导致                   | 参数变小导致                   |
|-------------|----------------------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| k-Means++   | 簇数k，容忍度               | 聚类更细，迭代更早终止          | 聚类更粗，迭代更多              |
| FCM         | 簇数k，模糊指数m            | 隶属度更模糊                  | 趋近硬划分                    |
| 凝聚层次聚类 | 目标簇数，链接方式          | 保留更多簇结构                 | 合并更激进                    |
| DBSCAN      | Eps，MinPts                | 簇更大，噪声点更少             | 簇更小，噪声点更多             |

## 参考链接
- 博客文章：[初识聚类算法:K均值、凝聚层次聚类和DBSCAN](http://blog.sina.com.cn/s/blog_62186b460101ard2.html)
- FCM算法详解：[模糊聚类FCM算法](http://www.sjsjw.com/kf_other/article/030919ABA018874.asp)
- 现代三语聚类教程仓库：[Clustering Playbook](https://github.com/HaoyuHu/clusterAnalysis)

---

![聚类示意图](/legacy/clustering-playbook/overview.png)

本文仍以2010年代的经典理论为基础，并衔接现代仓库示例，方便技术人员理解和实践聚类算法。
body.en:
## Introduction to Clustering
Clustering is an unsupervised learning method that partitions data into clusters where intra-cluster similarity is high while inter-cluster similarity is low. It is widely used for discovering intrinsic groupings in data.

### Key Definitions
- **Hierarchical vs Partitioning:** Hierarchical clustering organizes data into a tree structure representing nested clusters, while partitioning methods assign data into a fixed number of disjoint subsets.
- **Mutually Exclusive, Overlapping, and Fuzzy Clustering:** Points may belong strictly to one cluster (hard clustering) or partially (fuzzy clustering) with membership degrees summing to one.
- **Complete vs Partial Clustering:** Complete clustering assigns all points to clusters. Partial clustering allows for noise points that do not belong to any cluster.

### Cluster Types
- **Clearly Separated Clusters:** Inter-cluster distances are greater than any intra-cluster distances.
- **Prototype-based Clusters:** Each point is closer to its own cluster center than to other centers.
- **Graph-based Clusters:** Data points as graph nodes with edge weights representing similarity.
- **Density-based Clusters:** Clusters defined by regions with higher point density, effective for irregularly shaped clusters and noise detection.

## Algorithmic Families
| Algorithm               | Type                | Need prior k? | Noise-robust? | Handles arbitrary shapes? | Typical Complexity  | Suitable For                 |
|-------------------------|---------------------|---------------|---------------|---------------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------|
| **k-Means++**            | Partitioning         | Yes           | No            | Generally no              | Time O(nkt); Space O(n + k) | Well-separated, spherical clusters |
| **Fuzzy C-Means (FCM)**  | Soft Partitioning    | Yes           | No            | Generally no              | Time O(nkt); Space O(nk)    | Overlapping clusters with soft memberships |
| **Agglomerative Hierarchical** | Hierarchical     | No fixed k    | Limited       | Sometimes                | Time approx O(n^3); Space O(n^2) | Small datasets, hierarchical insight |
| **DBSCAN**               | Density-based        | No            | Yes           | Yes                       | Time approx O(n^2); Space O(n^2)  | Arbitrary shapes, noise detection |

## Detailed Algorithm Overviews

### 1. k-Means++ (Hard c-Means Clustering)
k-Means++ improves the initial selection of cluster centers to promote convergence to better minima than random initialization.

**Algorithm Steps:**
1. Choose the first center randomly.
2. Select subsequent centers with probability proportional to their squared distance from the nearest existing center.
3. Assign points to their nearest center.
4. Update centers based on cluster means.
5. Repeat assignment and update steps until centers stabilize.

**Pros:** Simple, fast, intuitive results for spherical clusters.

**Cons:** Requires pre-specified cluster count. Sensitive to noise and outliers.

**Core Python Snippet Example:**
```python
import random
import math

def distance_squared(p1, p2):
    return (p1[0] - p2[0])**2 + (p1[1] - p2[1])**2

def initialize_centers_pp(points, k):
    centers = [random.choice(points)]
    for _ in range(1, k):
        dist_sq = [min(distance_squared(p, c) for c in centers) for p in points]
        cumulative = [sum(dist_sq[:i+1]) for i in range(len(dist_sq))]
        r = random.uniform(0, cumulative[-1])
        for i, val in enumerate(cumulative):
            if val >= r:
                centers.append(points[i])
                break
    return centers
```

### 2. Fuzzy c-Means (FCM)
FCM generalizes k-means by allowing partial memberships between points and clusters via membership degrees, controlled by a fuzziness parameter `m > 1`.

**Workflow:**
- Initialize cluster centers (often using k-Means++).
- Calculate membership degrees with formula:

  \[ u_{i,j} = \left( \sum_{k=1}^c \left( \frac{d(x_j, v_i)}{d(x_j, v_k)} \right)^{\frac{2}{m-1}} \right)^{-1} \]

- Update cluster centers as weighted averages:

  \[ v_i = \frac{\sum_{j=1}^n u_{i,j}^m x_j}{\sum_{j=1}^n u_{i,j}^m} \]

- Repeat until convergence.

**Pros:** Captures overlapping clusters with soft memberships.

**Cons:** Like k-means, requires known cluster count and is sensitive to noise.

### 3. Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering
Starts with each point as a singleton cluster. Iteratively merges the two nearest clusters based on a linkage criterion (single, complete, average, or centroid distance) until a stopping criterion is met.

**Pros:** No need to pre-specify the number of clusters during merging; offers a dendrogram representing nested cluster hierarchies.

**Cons:** High computational complexity and memory usage; merging is irreversible and may produce noise clusters.

### 4. DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise)
DBSCAN clusters points based on density connectivity.

**Key parameters:**
- `eps`: radius for neighborhood search.
- `minPts`: minimum number of points in an eps-radius neighborhood to qualify as a core point.

**Point Types:**
- Core points: dense neighborhood.
- Border points: within neighborhood of a core point but not dense.
- Noise points: neither core nor border.

**Clustering process:**
- All core points connected via density reachable paths form clusters.
- Border points are assigned to their reachable core cluster.
- Noise points remain unassigned.

**Pros:** Handles noise and arbitrary-shaped clusters without specifying cluster count.

**Cons:** Parameter sensitivity; higher computational cost for large datasets.

## Summary of Parameter Impacts
| Algorithm                | Key Parameters                          | Larger Values Lead To                  | Smaller Values Lead To                |
|--------------------------|---------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| k-Means++                | Number of clusters, convergence tolerance | Finer partitioning, earlier stopping | Coarser clusters, longer convergence |
| Fuzzy C-Means            | Number of clusters, fuzziness parameter m  | Softer memberships                   | Approaches hard partitioning         |
| Agglomerative Hierarchical | Number of clusters, linkage criteria         | More clusters retained               | More aggressive merging              |
| DBSCAN                   | eps, minPts                           | Larger clusters, fewer noise points  | Smaller clusters, more noise points  |

## References
- Original blog posts: [K-Means, Hierarchical Clustering and DBSCAN](http://blog.sina.com.cn/s/blog_62186b460101ard2.html), [Fuzzy C-Means Algorithm](http://www.sjsjw.com/kf_other/article/030919ABA018874.asp)
- [Clustering Playbook: Modern Python tutorials](https://github.com/HaoyuHu/clusterAnalysis)

---

![Cluster Types and Algorithms](/legacy/clustering-playbook/overview.png)

This article reflects clustering fundamentals circa early 2010s with updates connecting to a modern educational repository featuring up-to-date implementations and extensions.

title.zh-CN: 聚类本质上是一个定义问题
title.en: Clustering Is Really a Definition Problem

summary.zh-CN: 聚类与其说是挑选某个知名算法，不如说是先定义“哪些点应当归在一起”究竟意味着什么。更新后的 `clustering-playbook` 的价值在于，它把这些彼此竞争的定义转化为一个可运行、可比较的决策系统。
summary.en: Clustering is less about picking a famous algorithm than about defining what it means for points to belong together. The updated `clustering-playbook` is valuable because it turns those competing definitions into a runnable, comparable decision system.

body.zh-CN:
# 聚类本质上是一个定义问题

> 聚类真正困难的部分，不是选择哪个库调用，而是决定你所说的“归为一类”到底是什么意思。

大多数入门材料都会把聚类介绍成一份算法菜单：K-Means++、模糊 C 均值、层次聚类、DBSCAN、OPTICS、谱聚类。从技术上说这没错，但从策略上看却很薄弱。在实践中，第一个决策并不是 _哪种算法更流行_，而是 _哪一种邻域、原型、密度或图结构的概念，才真正符合数据的生成过程_。

这也正是为什么重新整理后的 `clustering-playbook` 仓库比 2019 年最初那篇笔记更有用。旧文章对几种经典方法的讲解足以用于学习；而新的仓库则把它们变成了一个小型、可运行的决策系统：统一风格的 Python 脚本、共享工具函数、冒烟测试，以及在基于原型、基于密度、层次化和基于图的聚类视角之间更清晰的对比。

## 更好的思维模型

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[未标注样本] --> B{是什么让这些点归为一组？}
    B --> C[最近的中心\nK-Means++ / FCM]
    B --> D[合并路径\nAgglomerative]
    B --> E[稠密可达区域\nDBSCAN / OPTICS]
    B --> F[邻域图\nSpectral]
```

这张图之所以重要，是因为它把聚类从“算法选择”重新框定为“结构选择”。每个方法家族都蕴含着一种不同的世界观：

- **原型方法** 假设一个簇可以用一个中心来概括。
- **层次方法** 假设合并历史本身就携带信号。
- **密度方法** 假设簇是由稀疏空间分隔开的连通区域。
- **图方法** 假设局部邻域关系比全局几何形状更重要。

如果这种假设错了，参数调优就只是在做样子。

## 主要算法家族对比

| 算法家族 | 最适用场景 | 何时失效 | 主要权衡 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **K-Means++** | 已知 `k` 的紧凑、团块状簇 | 簇形弯曲、嵌套，或噪声较大 | 速度快、可解释性强，但几何偏见明显 |
| **模糊 C 均值** | 簇间重叠很重要，且软归属有价值 | 噪声较重或 `k` 未知 | 归属表达力更强，但同样带有基于中心的偏置 |
| **凝聚式层次聚类** | 需要从粗到细的层次结构 | 数据规模大，或早期合并会误导后续结构 | 层次丰富，但可扩展性较弱 |
| **DBSCAN / OPTICS** | 不规则形状和离群点都重要 | 密度差异极大且处理不当 | 能感知噪声，但参数更难直观设定 |
| **谱聚类** | 相似度图比到中心的距离更能捕捉结构 | 规模很大，或图构建不稳定 | 在非凸流形上很优雅，但代价高且敏感 |

真正的工程化思路，是把这张表当作约束地图，而不是流行度排行榜。

## 相较原始文章的改进

原始文章在算法讲解方面很扎实。它的局限也很典型，许多早期技术博客都有这个问题：它主要把算法当作彼此孤立的技巧来介绍。如今的仓库更好，因为它把这个主题变成了一个可供比较学习的平台。

有三项升级尤为重要：

1. **可运行的一致性。** 各个脚本共享 `--seed`、`--samples`、`--output` 和 `--show` 等参数，使比较不再停留在口头层面，而是成为可操作的实践。
2. **覆盖范围扩展。** 新增 OPTICS 和谱聚类补上了一个重要空白：并非所有硬聚类问题都是寻找中心的问题。
3. **仓库级质量。** 共享工具、冒烟测试和 CI 让这些内容更像可维护的工程知识，而不是一次性的随手笔记。

这种转变很微妙，却很重要。一篇好的技术文章会解释一种算法。一个更好的技术产物，还会教你如何比较、运行并证伪它。

## 实用选型经验法则

如果你只记住一条规则，那就记住这一条：

**根据你能容忍的失败模式来选择算法家族。**

- 如果可以接受把每个点都强制分到某个簇中，就从 **K-Means++** 开始。
- 如果模糊性本身就是问题的一部分，就转向 **模糊 C 均值（Fuzzy C-Means）**。
- 如果层次结构本身就有价值，就使用 **凝聚层次聚类（Agglomerative Clustering）**。
- 如果离群点是一等公民，就先从 **DBSCAN** 开始；当单一的全局密度阈值过于粗糙时，再升级到 **OPTICS**。
- 如果簇呈现出环形、月牙形或图社区这类结构，那么在把时间浪费在基于质心的方法上之前，先考虑 **谱聚类（Spectral Clustering）**。

第二条规则同样实用：**参数调优应该是在一个本来就说得通的认知框架上做细化，而不是去挽救一个从根上就错了的框架**。

## 从解释到使用

这个仓库简洁的命令行接口，是教学设计拿捏得当的一个好例子：

```bash
python3 k_means_plus_plus.py --samples 320 --clusters 4 --output k_means_plus_plus.png
python3 dbscan.py --samples 320 --eps 0.16 --min-samples 6 --output dbscan.png
python3 spectral_clustering.py --samples 320 --clusters 2 --neighbors 10 --output spectral.png
```

这很重要，因为它缩短了“我觉得这一类方法适合我的数据”和“我可以在几分钟内验证这个判断”之间的闭环。在应用机器学习中，更短的反馈回路通常比更花哨的理论更有价值。

## 总结视角

聚类常常被介绍为一种无监督分组。更好的描述是：它是在无标签数据上施加一种有用几何结构的艺术。

一旦你看清这一点，这个仓库就不再只是一个教程集合。它会成为一本精炼的实战手册，帮助你在选错算法之前，先问对问题。
body.en:
# Clustering Is Really a Definition Problem

> The hard part of clustering is not choosing a library call. It is deciding what you mean by "belong together".

Most introductions present clustering as a menu of algorithms: K-Means++, Fuzzy C-Means, Hierarchical Clustering, DBSCAN, OPTICS, Spectral Clustering. That is technically correct but strategically weak. In practice, the first decision is not _which algorithm is popular_, but _which notion of neighborhood, prototype, density, or graph structure matches the data-generating process_.

This is also why the refreshed `clustering-playbook` repository is more useful than the original 2019 note. The old article explained several classic methods well enough for study. The new repository turns them into a small, runnable decision system: consistent Python scripts, shared utilities, smoke tests, and a cleaner comparison across prototype-based, density-based, hierarchical, and graph-based views of a cluster.

## A Better Mental Model

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Unlabeled samples] --> B{What makes points belong together?}
    B --> C[Closest center\nK-Means++ / FCM]
    B --> D[Merge path\nAgglomerative]
    B --> E[Dense reachable region\nDBSCAN / OPTICS]
    B --> F[Neighborhood graph\nSpectral]
```

The diagram matters because it reframes clustering from "algorithm selection" to "structure selection." Each family encodes a different belief about the world:

- **Prototype methods** assume a cluster can be summarized by a center.
- **Hierarchical methods** assume the merge history itself carries signal.
- **Density methods** assume clusters are connected regions separated by sparse space.
- **Graph methods** assume local neighborhood relationships matter more than global geometry.

If that belief is wrong, parameter tuning becomes theater.

## Comparing the Main Families

| Family | Best fit | Fails when | Primary trade-off |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **K-Means++** | Compact, blob-like groups with known `k` | Shapes are curved, nested, or noisy | Fast and interpretable, but geometrically opinionated |
| **Fuzzy C-Means** | Overlap matters and soft membership is useful | Noise is heavy or `k` is unknown | More expressive assignments, same center-based bias |
| **Agglomerative** | You care about coarse-to-fine structure | Data is large or early merges mislead | Rich hierarchy, weak scalability |
| **DBSCAN / OPTICS** | Irregular shapes and outliers matter | Density varies wildly without careful handling | Noise-aware clustering, harder parameter intuition |
| **Spectral Clustering** | A similarity graph captures structure better than distance to a center | Scale is large or graph construction is unstable | Elegant on non-convex manifolds, expensive and sensitive |

The real engineering move is to treat this table as a constraint map, not a popularity ranking.

## What Improved Since the Original Article

The original post was strong on algorithm walkthroughs. Its limitation was common to many early technical blogs: it treated algorithms mostly as isolated techniques. The modern repository is better because it turns the subject into a comparative learning surface.

Three upgrades matter:

1. **Runnable consistency.** Scripts share parameters such as `--seed`, `--samples`, `--output`, and `--show`, which makes comparison operational rather than rhetorical.
2. **Expanded coverage.** Adding OPTICS and Spectral Clustering fixes an important gap: not all hard clustering problems are center-finding problems.
3. **Repository-level quality.** Shared utilities, smoke tests, and CI make the material feel like maintainable engineering knowledge rather than a one-off note.

That shift is subtle but important. A good technical article explains an algorithm. A better technical artifact also teaches how to compare, run, and falsify it.

## Practical Selection Heuristics

If you remember only one rule, remember this:

**Choose the algorithm family by the failure mode you can tolerate.**

- If forcing every point into a cluster is acceptable, start with **K-Means++**.
- If ambiguity is part of the problem, move to **Fuzzy C-Means**.
- If the hierarchy itself is valuable, use **Agglomerative Clustering**.
- If outliers are first-class citizens, begin with **DBSCAN** and escalate to **OPTICS** when one global density threshold is too crude.
- If clusters look like rings, moons, or graph communities, consider **Spectral Clustering** before wasting time on centroid methods.

A second rule is equally practical: **parameter tuning should refine an already plausible worldview, not rescue a broken one**.

## From Explanation to Use

The repository's small command-line surface is a good example of tasteful teaching design:

```bash
python3 k_means_plus_plus.py --samples 320 --clusters 4 --output k_means_plus_plus.png
python3 dbscan.py --samples 320 --eps 0.16 --min-samples 6 --output dbscan.png
python3 spectral_clustering.py --samples 320 --clusters 2 --neighbors 10 --output spectral.png
```

That matters because it shortens the loop between "I think this family matches my data" and "I can test that belief in minutes." In applied machine learning, shorter feedback loops usually beat more ornate theory.

## Closing View

Clustering is often introduced as unsupervised grouping. A better description is this: it is the art of imposing a useful geometry on unlabeled data.

Once you see that, the repository becomes more than a tutorial set. It becomes a compact playbook for asking the right question before choosing the wrong algorithm.

title.zh-CN: 解决GitHub Pages部署失败问题
title.en: Troubleshooting GitHub Pages Build Failures

summary.zh-CN: 本文总结了GitHub Pages部署失败的常见原因与解决方案，包括邮箱未认证、部署公钥限制、不支持的插件以及容量限制等，同时介绍了源配置和持续集成的注意事项，帮助确保网站稳定部署。
summary.en: This article outlines common causes and solutions for GitHub Pages build failures, from unverified emails and deploy key restrictions to unsupported plugins and size limits. It also covers source configuration and continuous integration considerations to ensure reliable site deployment.

body.zh-CN:
## 概述
GitHub Pages网站有时会在GitHub服务器上部署失败，并收到失败通知邮件。大部分失败邮件会明确指出出错文件及错误信息，但部分通用错误缺少详细诊断信息。本文为你梳理了排查GitHub Pages部署故障的系统方法。

---

## 常见故障原因

| 问题                      | 描述与解决方案                                                                                           |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **邮箱未认证**              | 只有经过邮箱认证的用户推送commit才会触发构建。请验证邮箱后重新推送，或联系GitHub支持手动触发构建。             |
| **使用部署公钥推送**        | 对组织的Pages仓库使用部署公钥推送不会触发构建。建议使用具备邮箱认证的机器用户（Machine User）加入组织进行推送。|
| **不支持的插件**            | GitHub Pages仅支持部分Jekyll插件，使用不支持的插件导致构建失败。请参考[Github文档](https://help.github.com/articles/using-jekyll-plugins-with-github-pages/)选择支持插件。  |
| **容量限制**                | 仓库和Pages网站均有1GB软性容量限制。超过限制可能导致无法部署，请裁减网站内容后重新尝试。                      |


## 源目录配置覆盖
我们的构建服务器会覆盖你在 `_config.yml` 文件中配置的 `source` 目录，因此修改该配置可能导致无法正常部署。


## 持续集成（CI）服务集成
部分CI服务如Travis CI，默认不会构建 `gh-pages` 分支。若希望CI自动构建该分支，需在配置文件中显式声明，例如在 `.travis.yml` 中添加：

```yaml
branches:
  only:
    - gh-pages
```

这样可确保CI监听并构建 `gh-pages` 分支，保持GitHub Pages网站的自动更新。

---

## 故障排查流程图

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[推送 Commit] --> B{邮箱是否认证?}
  B -- 否 --> C[认证邮箱并重新推送]
  B -- 是 --> D{是否使用部署公钥推送?}
  D -- 是 --> E[使用机器用户推送]
  D -- 否 --> F{是否使用不支持的插件?}
  F -- 是 --> G[移除不支持的插件]
  F -- 否 --> H{容量是否超限?}
  H -- 是 --> I[减小网站体积]
  H -- 否 --> J[构建成功]

```

---

通过这个清晰的流程指导，可以有效定位并解决GitHub Pages的构建问题，提升部署成功率。
body.en:
## Overview
GitHub Pages sites sometimes fail to build on GitHub's servers, triggering a failure notification email. Many failure notices pinpoint problematic files and errors, but some generic failures lack detailed diagnostic information. This guide provides a structured approach to troubleshooting your GitHub Pages build issues.

---

## Common Causes of Build Failures

| Issue                      | Description & Solution                                                                                   |
|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Unverified Email Address**  | Builds only trigger when commits come from users with verified email addresses. Verify your email, then push again. Alternatively, contact GitHub Support to trigger a manual build. |
| **Deploy Key Pushes**         | Pushing with a deploy key cannot trigger builds on organization Pages repositories. Use a machine user with verified email as an organization member instead. |
| **Unsupported Plugins**       | GitHub Pages supports only a subset of Jekyll plugins. Using unsupported plugins causes builds to fail. Reference [Using Jekyll Plugins with GitHub Pages](https://help.github.com/articles/using-jekyll-plugins-with-github-pages/) for approved plugins and usage. |
| **Repository Size Limits**    | Both repositories and Pages sites have a soft size limit of 1 GB. Exceeding this may prevent builds. Reduce site size accordingly before retrying. |


## Source Configuration Overrides
Our build server enforces its own `source` setting during site generation, overriding any changes you make to the `source` option in `_config.yml`. Customizing `source` in your config file may cause your site to fail building.


## Integrating with Continuous Integration (CI) Services
Some CI tools, such as Travis CI, do not run builds on the `gh-pages` branch by default. To automate site builds as part of your CI pipeline, ensure your CI configuration explicitly includes `gh-pages`. For example, add this snippet to your `.travis.yml`:

```yaml
branches:
  only:
    - gh-pages
```

This ensures your CI system processes pushes to the `gh-pages` branch, keeping your GitHub Pages site in sync.

---

## Troubleshooting Workflow Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Push Commit] --> B{Is Email Verified?}
  B -- No --> C[Verify Email & Push Again]
  B -- Yes --> D{Push via Deploy Key?}
  D -- Yes --> E[Use Machine User Instead]
  D -- No --> F{Unsupported Plugin?}
  F -- Yes --> G[Remove Unsupported Plugins]
  F -- No --> H{Site Size & Quota OK?}
  H -- No --> I[Reduce Site Size]
  H -- Yes --> J[Build Succeeds]

```

---

This structured approach should help you diagnose and fix most GitHub Pages build issues efficiently.

title.zh-CN: Tutu-Android：清华大学自习室预约助手
title.en: Tutu-Android: A Study Room Booking Assistant for Tsinghua University

summary.zh-CN: 面向清华大学人文图书馆的 Android 预约助手，结合智能预约、后台轮询、实时余座查询与提醒操作，降低预约摩擦，并帮助避免爽约处罚。
